message:
feat: Add external dependency resolution for cross-project coordination

Implement capability-based external dependency tracking that enables
issues to depend on work in other beads projects. External dependencies
use the format "external:<project>:<capability>" and are satisfied when
the target project has a closed issue with label "provides:<capability>".

## Configuration

External projects are declared in config.yaml:
```yaml
external_projects:
  backend: /path/to/backend/project
  auth-service: ../auth-service
```

Paths can be absolute or relative to the project root.

## Core Implementation (src/storage/sqlite.rs)

New functions for external dependency resolution:
- list_external_dependency_ids(): Query dependencies matching "external:%"
- parse_external_dependency(): Parse "external:project:capability" format
- query_external_project_capabilities(): Open external DB read-only and
  check for closed issues with "provides:X" labels
- resolve_external_dependency_statuses(): Batch resolve all external deps
  to satisfied/unsatisfied status
- external_blockers(): Compute blocking status including parent-child
  propagation (children of externally-blocked parents are also blocked)

Also includes:
- Updated delete_issue() to accept optional timestamp parameter
- Clippy fix: use is_some_and() instead of map().unwrap_or(false)
- Moved const SQLITE_VAR_LIMIT before statements to satisfy clippy
- Use deterministic timestamps in tests for reproducibility
- Exclude external: deps from blocked_issues_cache (evaluated at runtime)

## Config Layer (src/config/mod.rs)

- external_projects_from_layer(): Parse external_projects config section
- external_project_db_paths(): Resolve project paths to database files
- Support both external_projects and external-projects key formats
- Add external-projects to startup keys for early loading

## CLI Integration

blocked.rs:
- Load external project config and compute external blockers
- Merge external blockers into blocked issues list
- Handle issues blocked transitively through parent-child relationships
- Fix blocker ID parsing to handle external: prefix correctly using
  rsplit_once() instead of split(':').next()

dep.rs:
- Pass external_db_paths to dep_tree command
- Render external dependency nodes with status indicators:
  - ⏳ prefix for unsatisfied (blocked) external deps
  - ✓ prefix for satisfied (closed) external deps
- Show project:capability as the node title
- Prevent tree expansion into external nodes (they have no sub-deps)

ready.rs:
- Filter out issues blocked by external dependencies from ready list
- Compute external blockers at query time for accurate results

## Tests

e2e_ready.rs - ready_respects_external_dependencies():
- Create main project with issue depending on external:extproj:auth
- Verify issue is blocked when external dep unsatisfied
- Create provider issue in external project with provides:auth label
- Close provider and verify main issue becomes ready

e2e_relations.rs - e2e_dep_tree_external_nodes():
- Verify external nodes appear in dependency tree
- Check status transitions from blocked -> closed
- Validate ⏳/✓ prefix markers

sqlite.rs unit tests:
- test_external_dependency_satisfied_not_blocking
- test_external_dependency_blocks_and_propagates_to_children

## Design Decisions

1. **Capability-based**: Dependencies reference capabilities, not specific
   issue IDs. This allows the external project to reorganize work without
   breaking dependencies.

2. **Label-based resolution**: "provides:X" labels create a many-to-one
   mapping where any closed issue can satisfy the capability.

3. **Read-only access**: External databases are opened SQLITE_OPEN_READ_ONLY
   to prevent any accidental modifications.

4. **Query-time evaluation**: External deps are not cached in
   blocked_issues_cache; they're evaluated fresh on each query to reflect
   current external project state.

5. **Parent-child propagation**: If a parent issue is externally blocked,
   all child issues inherit that blocking status.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
